Do: Make all interactive elements at least 48x48dp/pt
Dont: Use touch targets smaller than 44dp/pt
Reason: Small targets cause mis-taps and fail WCAG 2.5.5; 48dp covers finger pad size for 95th percentile

Do: Add at least 8dp spacing between adjacent touch targets
Dont: Place tappable elements edge-to-edge without spacing
Reason: Adjacent targets without gaps cause accidental activation; Material and HIG both recommend 8dp minimum

Do: Use UIKit hitTest expansion or contentEdgeInsets for small icons
Dont: Rely solely on the visual size of SF Symbols for tap area
Reason: SF Symbols render small; HIG requires the tappable area to be at least 44pt regardless of icon size

Do: Use consistent navigation patterns throughout the app
Dont: Mix bottom tabs with hamburger menus and side drawers in the same app
Reason: Inconsistent navigation increases cognitive load and user disorientation; pick one primary pattern

Do: Use NavigationBar with 3-5 destinations for top-level navigation
Dont: Use more than 5 items in bottom navigation
Reason: Material Design 3 specifies 3-5 destinations; more items compress touch targets and overwhelm users

Do: Support swipe-back gesture on all pushed screens
Dont: Override or disable interactivePopGestureRecognizer
Reason: Disabling swipe-back breaks fundamental iOS muscle memory; HIG mandates edge-swipe for navigation stacks

Do: Provide immediate visual feedback within 100ms of user interaction
Dont: Leave buttons in their default state during async operations
Reason: Users perceive >100ms delays as lag; show loading/disabled state instantly then resolve with success/error

Do: Use Material ripple effect on all clickable surfaces
Dont: Use only color change or no feedback on tap
Reason: Ripple is the expected Android interaction pattern; omitting it makes the app feel non-native

Do: Use haptic feedback (UIImpactFeedbackGenerator) for meaningful actions
Dont: Add haptics to every tap — reserve for significant moments
Reason: HIG recommends haptics for confirmations and state changes; overuse desensitizes and annoys users

Do: Show skeleton screens for content loading instead of spinners
Dont: Show a blank screen or only a centered spinner during data load
Reason: Skeleton screens reduce perceived wait time by 35%; users understand the layout before content arrives

Do: Implement optimistic UI updates for user-initiated actions
Dont: Wait for server confirmation before updating the UI for simple actions
Reason: Optimistic updates make the app feel instant; roll back gracefully on failure with undo option

Do: Use Shimmer package for skeleton loading animations
Dont: Build custom animation controllers for simple loading placeholders
Reason: Shimmer package handles performant GPU-based animation; custom solutions risk janky frames on low-end devices

Do: Show actionable error messages with a retry button
Dont: Display raw error codes or stack traces to users
Reason: Users cannot interpret technical errors; always provide a human-readable message and a clear recovery path

Do: Implement exponential backoff for automatic retries
Dont: Retry failed network requests in a tight loop
Reason: Tight loops can DDoS your own backend and drain battery; exponential backoff with jitter is industry standard

Do: Use UIAlertController for critical errors that block the flow
Dont: Show errors only as inline text that users can scroll past
Reason: Critical errors demand user acknowledgment; alerts ensure the error is seen before the user can proceed

Do: Support Dynamic Type / system font scaling up to 200%
Dont: Use fixed font sizes that ignore user accessibility preferences
Reason: 15% of users adjust text size; ignoring system scale violates WCAG 1.4.4 and platform accessibility guidelines

Do: Ensure all images have contentDescription (Android) or accessibilityLabel (iOS/Flutter)
Dont: Leave decorative and informational images without alt text
Reason: Screen readers announce images without labels as 'image'; meaningful descriptions enable blind users to understand content

Do: Use Modifier.semantics and Modifier.clearAndSetSemantics in Compose
Dont: Rely only on default TalkBack behavior without explicit semantics
Reason: Custom composables need explicit semantics for TalkBack to announce them correctly; Material components handle this automatically

Do: Provide a visible button alternative for every swipe or long-press action
Dont: Hide destructive actions behind gestures without any visible alternative
Reason: Gesture-only actions are undiscoverable; users with motor impairments cannot perform complex gestures reliably

Do: Support standard iOS gestures: swipe-to-delete and pull-to-refresh
Dont: Implement custom gesture recognizers that conflict with system gestures
Reason: Conflicting gestures cause unpredictable behavior; use UISwipeActionsConfiguration and UIRefreshControl

Do: Use Modifier.swipeable or AnchoredDraggable for dismiss gestures
Dont: Implement raw pointer/touch handling for swipe-to-dismiss
Reason: Framework swipeable handles velocity thresholds and snap animations; raw touch math misses edge cases

Do: Limit to 2 font families maximum across the entire app
Dont: Use more than 3 different font families in a single app
Reason: Multiple fonts create visual noise and increase bundle size; one serif + one sans-serif is the practical maximum

Do: Keep body text between 14sp-18sp for comfortable mobile reading
Dont: Set body text below 12sp or above 22sp on phone screens
Reason: Below 12sp strains eyes; above 22sp wastes space and breaks line rhythm; 16sp is the optimal default

Do: Load Google Fonts asynchronously and provide a fallback system font
Dont: Bundle large font files directly in assets without lazy loading
Reason: Bundled fonts increase APK/IPA size by 200-500KB per weight; Google Fonts package caches after first download

Do: Test all color combinations for WCAG AA 4.5:1 contrast ratio
Dont: Choose colors based solely on aesthetics without contrast verification
Reason: 1 in 12 men has color vision deficiency; low contrast fails WCAG 2.1 AA and excludes visually impaired users

Do: Provide a dark mode theme that uses OLED-friendly true black backgrounds
Dont: Use the same color palette for both light and dark themes
Reason: Dark mode reduces eye strain and saves battery on OLED screens; colors need re-mapping not just inversion

Do: Use color plus icon/shape to convey status — never color alone
Dont: Rely solely on red/green color coding for error/success states
Reason: Color-blind users cannot distinguish red from green; adding icons (checkmark/X) makes status universally readable

Do: Keep animations under 300ms for transitions and 150ms for micro-interactions
Dont: Use animations longer than 500ms for common UI transitions
Reason: Long animations feel sluggish and block user flow; Material Motion and HIG both target 250-350ms for page transitions

Do: Respect prefers-reduced-motion / Reduce Motion accessibility setting
Dont: Force animations even when the user has requested reduced motion
Reason: Vestibular disorders cause nausea with motion; ignoring this setting violates WCAG 2.3.3 and platform guidelines

Do: Use AnimatedContainer and ImplicitlyAnimatedWidget for simple transitions
Dont: Use explicit AnimationController for every animation including simple ones
Reason: Implicit animations are simpler and less error-prone; reserve AnimationController for complex choreographed sequences
